title: allamanda oenotherifolia
intro: Allamanda oenotherifolia, also known as the yellow allamanda or golden trumpet, is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It is a shrub-like plant native to Central and South America, known for its bright yellow, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Allamanda oenotherifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, making it a valuable remedy in natural medicine.
